# El Cid — Historic Homes in West Palm Beach

> El Cid is a historic residential neighborhood in West Palm Beach, Florida, developed during the 1920s Florida land boom and listed on the National Register of Historic Places. This is the official neighborhood site for buyers and sellers of El Cid homes, presented by the Modern Living Group team at Compass. Listings are sourced live from BeachesMLS and updated daily.

El Cid (West Palm Beach, FL 33401) is one of the city's most prestigious historic neighborhoods, known for its Mediterranean Revival and Spanish Mission-style homes on tree-lined streets along the western shore of the Intracoastal Waterway — directly across from Palm Beach and just south of Downtown West Palm Beach. The district contains 281 historic buildings and is bounded by Flamingo Drive (north), South Flagler Drive (east), Dyer Road (south) and South Dixie Highway (west). It was listed on the National Register of Historic Places on August 31, 1995, and is also a locally designated historic district (1993).

This site is operated by Modern Living Group at Compass. Patrick Lafferty leads the team and is the El Cid specialist, having sold Downtown West Palm Beach real estate since 2008. Contact: (561) 228-8420, info@modernlivingre.com. Modern Living Group agents are licensed Florida real-estate salespersons affiliated with Compass Florida, LLC, an Equal Housing Opportunity broker.

Property data is sourced from BeachesMLS under IDX rules and is deemed reliable but not guaranteed; verify with a Modern Living Group agent before acting.

## Key pages

- [Home](https://elcidhomes.com/): neighborhood overview, featured homes, history, recent sales, and the El Cid specialist.
- [For Sale](https://elcidhomes.com/for-sale): current El Cid homes for sale, updated daily from BeachesMLS.
- [For Rent](https://elcidhomes.com/for-rent): current El Cid homes for rent.
- [Recent Sales](https://elcidhomes.com/recent-sales): closed El Cid home sales with real sold prices, dates, and price-per-square-foot comps.
- [Selling](https://elcidhomes.com/selling): free El Cid home valuation and the team's marketing approach for historic homes.
- [Contact](https://elcidhomes.com/contact): reach the Modern Living Group team for showings, valuations, or questions.

## Neighborhood facts

- Name: El Cid (also "El Cid Historic District")
- Location: West Palm Beach, Palm Beach County, Florida 33401; western shore of the Intracoastal Waterway, directly across from Palm Beach, just south of Downtown West Palm Beach
- Boundaries: Flamingo Drive, South Flagler Drive, Dyer Road, South Dixie Highway
- Type: historic single-family residential neighborhood; 281 historic buildings
- Architecture: predominantly Mediterranean Revival, with Mission, Monterey, Colonial Revival, bungalow and Art Deco examples; stucco walls, barrel-tile roofs, arches, courtyards and wrought-iron details. Prominent 1920s architects included Maurice Fatio and Howard Major.
- History: subdivided from old pineapple fields in the 1920s by Pittsburgh socialite Jay Phipps and named for El Cid, the Spanish hero who conquered Valencia in 1094; most homes built between the early 1920s and 1945.
- Designation: National Register of Historic Places (August 31, 1995); local historic district (1993). Exterior changes generally require a Certificate of Appropriateness from the City of West Palm Beach.
- Nearby: Norton Museum of Art, Ann Norton Sculpture Gardens, Armory Art Center, Antique Row on South Dixie Highway, the West Palm Beach GreenMarket, and the Brightline West Palm Beach station.
